name: basis-rollup
title: L1 Rollup Contract for Enterprise zkEVM L2 with Block-Level State Tracking
hypothesis: A Solidity rollup contract can verify validity proofs of L2 batches, maintain per-enterprise state root chains, and process batch submissions at < 500K gas, extending Validium RU-V3 patterns to the full zkEVM model with block-level tracking and commit-prove-execute lifecycle.
null_hypothesis: The commit-prove-execute pattern introduces sufficient storage and call overhead that total gas per batch exceeds 500K, making the multi-phase approach infeasible without sacrificing on-chain data or safety invariants.

predictions.statement: Commit phase uses < 120K gas (batch metadata + L2 block range storage)
predictions.rationale: 2-3 new storage slots (66-88K) + calldata + event emission

predictions.statement: Prove phase uses < 250K gas (ZK verification + proof linkage)
predictions.rationale: Groth16 pairing check ~205K + minimal storage update ~20K

predictions.statement: Total gas across all three phases < 500K per batch
predictions.rationale: Sum of P1+P2+P3 predictions: ~450K with margin

predictions.statement: All validium safety invariants (ChainContinuity, ProofBeforeState, NoGap, EnterpriseIsolation) are preserved in the extended model
predictions.rationale: Structural enforcement via auto-incrementing IDs and per-enterprise mappings
